title: The Plunder.
content:
I am of all bereft,
    Save but some few beans left,
    Whereof, at last, to make
    For me and mine a cake,
    Which eaten, they and I
    Will say our grace, and die.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "The Plunder.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
